title: Why Your Money Is Losing Value Every Year — Inflation Explained for Indians
summary: Keeping money in a savings account? You're losing purchasing power every year. Here's inflation explained simply — and what to do about it.
content:
# Why Your Money Is Losing Value Every Year

Your savings account shows ₹5,00,000 today. Three years later it shows ₹5,15,000 — but you can buy significantly less. This is inflation.

## What Is Inflation?

Inflation is the rate at which prices rise over time. India measures it as the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

**India's historical CPI average: ~5.5–6.5% per year**

Use our [Inflation Calculator](/tools/inflation-calculator.html) to see how money loses purchasing power.

## The Real Impact: ₹10 Lakh Over Time

| Year | Nominal Amount | Real Value (6% inflation) |
|---|---|---|
| Today | ₹10,00,000 | ₹10,00,000 |
| 5 years | ₹10,00,000 | ₹7,47,258 |
| 10 years | ₹10,00,000 | ₹5,58,395 |
| 20 years | ₹10,00,000 | ₹3,11,805 |

## The Savings Account Problem

- Savings account interest: 3.5%
- Inflation: 6%
- **Real return: −2.5%** — you are losing purchasing power

## Investments That Beat Inflation in India

| Investment | Returns | Real Return vs 6% Inflation |
|---|---|---|
| Equity Mutual Funds | 10–14% CAGR | +4–8% ✅ |
| Gold | ~8–10% | +2–4% ✅ |
| PPF | 7.1% | ~+1% ✅ |
| FD (post-tax 30% slab) | ~5% | −1% ❌ |
| Savings Account | 3.5% | −2.5% ❌ |

## The Minimum Goal

Your portfolio should beat inflation after tax. For most Indians:
- Emergency fund: sweep-in FD
- Long-term wealth: equity mutual fund SIPs
- Tax saving: ELSS SIP (80C benefit)

*Disclaimer: Past returns don't guarantee future performance. Consult a SEBI-registered advisor.*
